topic: OpenAI Codex for Everything: April 2026 Desktop Update Full Review

key_points: Covers Computer Use, in-app browser, and 90+ plugins in the April 2026 update
Focuses on developer workflow improvements like PR reviews and multi-terminal support
Explains how background computer use works without taking over user's screen
Full review of Codex computer use, memory preview, and plugin ecosystem
Covers how Codex memory stores preferences and project-specific knowledge across sessions
Discusses enterprise growth: 6x Codex users in ChatGPT Business/Enterprise Jan–Apr 2026
Positions Codex as the strongest Claude Code rival after April 2026 update
Highlights PR review feature as key differentiator for engineering teams
Covers image generation capability using gpt-image-1.5 within same workflow
Compares Codex (GPT-5.5, asynchronous sandbox VMs) vs Claude Code (Opus 4.7, terminal-native)
Claude Code leads SWE-bench at 80.9%; Codex close at ~80%; Codex wins Terminal-Bench 2.0 at 77.3%
Recommends Codex for background automated workflows, Claude Code for complex refactoring
Three-way comparison: Codex (cloud async agent), Cursor (AI-native IDE), Claude Code (terminal agent)
Most high-velocity teams use at least two: Cursor for daily work, Codex for automated workflows
Cursor $20/month remains cheapest for IDE; Codex rides ChatGPT Plus at $20/month

stats.fact: OpenAI Codex reached 4 million weekly developer users as of April 21, 2026, up from 3 million just five days earlier at launch
Codex CLI npm downloads grew from 82,000 in April 2025 to 14.53 million in March 2026 — a 177x increase in one year
Codex users in ChatGPT Business and Enterprise grew 6x between January and April 2026
OpenAI Codex scores ~80% on SWE-bench Verified, close to Claude Code's leading 80.9%
Codex wins Terminal-Bench 2.0 at 77.3%, reflecting OpenAI's focus on agentic terminal execution
The April 2026 update shipped 90+ new plugins at launch including Atlassian Rovo, CircleCI, Microsoft 365 suite, Salesforce, HubSpot, and Linear
GPT-5.5 (Codex's underlying model) API pricing starts at $5/1M input tokens and $30/1M output tokens
OpenAI named a Leader in 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Enterprise AI Coding Agents

angles: The 'Codex for Everything' framing: OpenAI's bet that one agent should handle your entire dev workflow — not just code
Background computer use as a paradigm shift: agents that run in parallel without taking over your screen
From CLI to desktop command center: how Codex evolved from a code-completion API to a full agentic platform in 12 months
The plugin ecosystem play: 90+ integrations positions Codex as Jira+Slack+GitHub+IDE in one agent
Memory as the missing link: why persistent context across sessions changes how teams adopt AI coding tools
Asynchronous coding: the case for assigning tasks to Codex like a junior developer and reviewing PRs later
Enterprise angle: 6x user growth in Business/Enterprise shows Codex is becoming a team-wide tool, not just a solo dev toy
Benchmarks reality check: Codex and Claude Code are nearly tied — the real differentiator is workflow fit, not benchmark scores
outline: H2: What Is 'Codex for (Almost) Everything'? The April 16, 2026 Update Explained
H2: Background Computer Use: Codex Sees Your Screen and Works Alongside You
H2: In-App Browser: Iterating on Frontend and Game Designs Without Switching Tools

H2: Memory and Goals: Persistent Context Across Sessions and Repeatable Automations
H2: PR Reviews, Multi-Terminal Tabs, and SSH Remote DevBox Support
H2: Image Generation with GPT-Image-1.5 Inside Codex
H2: Benchmarks — SWE-bench ~80%, Terminal-Bench 77.3%: How Codex Compares to Claude Code and Cursor
H2: Pricing: Codex Access with ChatGPT Plus ($20/mo), Pro ($200/mo), and Enterprise
H2: Who Should Use Codex vs Claude Code vs Cursor in 2026?
